title: Greetings
description: Master hellos, farewells, and asking how someone is.
openerZafar: A traveler seeks to learn the tongue of the ancients! Let us begin where all journeys begin — with a proper greeting. How would you greet me in Arabic?
openerQamar: So. Greetings. The very first thing every child learns. Even my desert fox knows مرحبا. Can YOU manage that much, or shall we start with pointing and grunting?
openerJasmine: أهلاً بك — welcome, dear student. The word مرحبا is like a jasmine blossom — small, but it fills the whole room with warmth. Shall we learn how to greet the world in Arabic?
openerTariq: مرحبا! Every journey starts with a greeting — even in the desert, the first word to a stranger can save your life. Let us begin there. Do you know how to say 'hello' in Arabic?
openerFaris: أهلاً يا بطل! Welcome to the squad, champion! Before every match the captains shake hands. مرحبا — that's your first touch on the ball. Greet me back in Arabic and we kick off!

title: Numbers
description: Count from 1 to 10, tell someone your age, talk about quantities.
openerZafar: Numbers! The language of the stars and the desert trade routes. I counted the grains of sand in my lamp for 1,000 years. Now tell me — what is the Arabic word for 'one'? Is it واحد? Let us see if you know.
openerQamar: Numbers. How thrilling. I'll ask you something — how many years did it take you to find this app? Count them for me in Arabic if you can manage even that. Start with واحد — 'one'. Go on.
openerJasmine: واحد، اثنان، ثلاثة — numbers are the heartbeat of language. The great poets counted their syllables before they counted their gold. Tell me — can you count to three in Arabic?
openerTariq: واحد — one star is enough to find north. اثنان — two stars form a line. Can you continue? The desert has ten stars worth knowing. Let us start counting together.
openerFaris: Numbers win matches! My jersey is رقم عشرة — number ten. واحد، اثنان، ثلاثة — count the goals with me! Can you take it from ثلاثة? Three — what comes next, champion?

title: Market Scene
description: Buy items, ask prices, describe things by color and size.
openerZafar: Ah, the souk! The great bazaar where I spent many happy centuries before the curse. You wish to haggle and trade? Then you must know the words. Tell me — how would you ask for the price of something in Arabic?
openerQamar: The market! If you can't ask بكم هذا — 'how much is this?' — you'll pay triple price for everything. Let's make sure that doesn't happen. Impress me. Ask me the price of something.
openerJasmine: في السوق — the market is where all of life happens. I used to sneak away from the palace just to smell the spices and hear the merchants call. بكم هذا؟ — can you ask how much something costs in Arabic?
openerTariq: السوق! Every town on every trade route has one. I've bartered camels for water and stars for stories. The first word you need is بكم — 'how much'. Do you know it? Try asking me the price of something.
openerFaris: The market is like the transfer window — everyone's haggling! بكم هذا؟ — 'how much is this?' Ask me that in Arabic and I'll teach you to negotiate like a striker chasing a contract.

title: World Cup
description: Mundial Edition! Football vocabulary — goals, teams, the trophy, and the roar of the crowd.
openerZafar: كأس العالم! Even sealed in my lamp, I heard the roar of crowds across a thousand years. Football — كرة القدم — unites every tongue. Tell me, what is the Arabic word for 'goal'?
openerQamar: The World Cup. Twenty-two adults chasing one ball while millions scream. Riveting. Still — هدف means 'goal', and if you can't even say that, don't expect a trophy. Try it.
openerJasmine: كأس العالم — the World Cup! Even the poets would pause their verses for a beautiful goal. هدف — say it, and feel the whole stadium rise. Can you tell me 'goal' in Arabic?
openerTariq: كأس العالم! The whole desert gathers around one radio for the match. هدف — 'goal!' — is the word that makes grown men weep with joy. Can you shout it in Arabic?
openerFaris: هيا بنا! THIS is my world — كأس العالم, the World Cup! I've lifted the كأس myself, champion. Let's start with the sweetest word in the game: هدف — 'GOAL!' Can you say it in Arabic?
